text: Guide to Achieving Fector's Challenge! Just got this achievement today, realized that there was a trick you could use to beat it and felt like sharing it! So if you were like me and have been playing for years without getting this achievement, then gather round. The core of this is that your save for Journey of the Prairie King is connected to your save for your farm. Thus, the following process can get you through Journey of the Prairie King 'Without Dying'. Beat JotPK normally to get an arcade cabinet in your house. Wake up and attempt the prairie levels. Get a good amount of cash plus upgrades without dying and get to the next world. Quit out, finish your Stardew day, save, continue, and if you die, quit to title and restart the day to reset the death. Repeat for forest levels and cursed lands. Get as many attempts at Fector as needed. Hope this is helpful for some folks!
label: Showcase

text: skipped green rain — how to edit files? This is a long shot, but is it possible to edit my game file so that I get a second green rain? Basically, I'm playing with my mother the first time. We got the weather alert on TV that green rain was incoming, and I was thrilled. She asked if we could have a short pause so she could go get coffee IRL, and I said sure. Being new to the game, she clicked through the profit-summary screen, so I didn't, trying to prevent the new day from starting. Tragically, I lost connection and didn't notice because my window was minimized as I waited. Because I was gone, her game continued to the next day without pausing. It cycled through and had her pass out at 2AM, and because nothing happened that day, it went onward to the next. Worst news: progress saved. So basically, we missed the green rain because we paused the game wrong. I love the green rain, and Year 1 is always special too. I really want to know if there's some way to change the game file to say I haven't had green rain yet, or change the date that it's meant to occur to a future date? It's only on the 8th of the month, so there's plenty of eligible dates. TLDR: I want to tell my game file that we didn't have green rain yet in Year 1 because I paused the game wrong and it cycled past the day Any help would be appreciated. :( <3
label: Help/Question

text: How good is the translation of Stardew Valley in other languages? I can't recomend it to learn Spanish, for example. Even if 80% of the translation is excelent, another 10% is lacking and the other 10% is so bad I honestly don't think a professional agency or translator worked on it. Not saying this out of spite or anything, the translation in some parts is beyond bad, or there is directly no translation, with the original English showing. A small example is the issue with the dehydrator. The translation should be something akin of "Hongos morados deshidratados", instead it is "La seta lila se ha deshidratado", which translates back to "The purple mushroom has been dehydrated". "purple" and "mushroom" can be translated as "lila" and "seta", although they would not be my choices, but as you can see, it is far from a correct translation, and I suspect machine translation has been used for a lot of the game. Complains appart (I would love to do an in depth post about this, but it would be really long, since I've been working as translator for a really long time now), I don't recomend the Spanish translation. Are there similar problems with the other languages? How good are the translations?
label: Discussion/Opinion

text: In defence of pineapples I totally understand the starfruit vs Ancient fruit debate in terms of input/output value. But I love growing pineapples as a substitute for both. I hate replanting starfruit and restocking, even using a seed maker seems to take longer to build up seeds. I decided to do Ancient fruit in the greenhouse and that was fine, but I also hate waiting for the wine to age. (I hate ageing in general). Plus it just takes longer to build a huge crop of them. I know pineapples are also only accessible via the island, but once you get a decent crop going on the farm there, it’s not a bad way to make a steady amount of profit even if it’s not copious amounts. I also don’t mind making jelly out of them while I don’t think ancient fruit or starfruit jelly is the best output for those, I try to stick to making wine with them. Making pineapple wine is completely okay as well, base it’s 900 bucks a bottle, and I always go artisan for that reason so 1260 a bottle for me isn’t a bad profit off free seeds. And once I had enough crops for dehydrators, I was doing pretty well shipping about 100 jars of them per week, and I’m fine with making 100,000 more often than waiting two months for half a million off wine. I feel like they’re a good, easy compromise, plus they’re free once you’re getting golden coconuts and monster drops from the volcano. I just set up a bunch of seed makers and gradually cover the island farm with them. I know ancient seeds are free as well but they’re designed to be difficult to build up crop wise. If you genuinely hate replanting and have trouble getting ancient fruit crops going, pineapples would be the way to go. And planting mixed seeds on the island has a chance of growing pineapples as well. They literally cost nothing to buy, are easier to find and not that hard to get going in terms of farming.
label: Discussion/Opinion

text: Well, I settled on my next challenge farm. Keeping it simple. Blueberries to perfection. I've done every manner of bizarre artisan good, so screw it. Simple playthrough, only blueberries. Only allowed to buy blueberry seeds, only allowed to sell blueberries. Obviously year 1 will be pointless but as long as I can sort out 3-400 garden pots and a bunch of sheds before the end of the first Summer with my limited profits I'll be hoping for perfection around year 4. Clay is going to be the huge bottle neck between garden pots and eventually deluxe retaining soil though. Obviously we will be primarily growing in sheds given the whole seasonal aspect of the game. Edit, this is only selling blueberries for perfection. This means NO other profit. Entirely Joja, really just earning a way to success. I've played too many farms to enjoy making friends and going through those paces. This is just a quick way to kill 15 minutes a few times a day, and I expect to end with a few thousand blueberry plants making a quick run to perfection*

text: Craft Master Achievement Checklist. Hi!! I’ve been a long time casual Stardew player on my switch. I enjoy using/making checklists, and I thought I would share one for the Craft Master achievement. While the new 1.6 items haven’t been implemented on console yet, they have been included on this checklist! I used the Stardew Valley Wiki page to compile all the numbers and item icons. I don’t really have a graphic design background, so it’s not the prettiest, but I think it looks better than just a plain list :’) On another note, you should be able to make one of everything using these numbers (a jack o lantern requires a pumpkin and a torch, so the wood and sap used in making a torch is counted twice). This also applies to items like the heavy furnace. You should be able to make a total of three furnaces, two of which would be used in crafting the heavy furnace. I hope that makes sense! The only thing that isn’t included are the wild seeds used in making tea leaves. Although, you’ll be making each season of wild seeds already and can use those towards the tea leaves! I hope everything makes sense and that the list will be helpful to someone :)
label: Showcase
